item.title: Classification using SVM: Beginner's Tale in R
item.description: This is literally the first time I’ve ever tried using SVM. I’m not so big on all this “Machine Learning” thing; most of the time I use R merely for data analysis, statistics, and visualizations. But after careful reading it seems that SVM could be really useful for quick data classification, especially for predicting if the result is going to be binary (Yes or No, A or B, 1 or 0, etc.

item.title: How to download Google Forms' survey responses as CSV file
item.description: Intro Collecting data is undoubtedly one of the most important parts in any data science workflow. One of the best easiest ways to collect data, especially from people, is through survey. And among the survey-making platforms, Google Forms is particular because it is free, its ease of use, and overall very direct. It is perhaps not the best, or with the most feature, but it gets the jobs done.

item.title: How to plot a linear regression in R
item.description: Intro A more compatible title perhaps would be: How to plot an Excel-like linear regression in R. This is a half-note, half-tutorial that I wrote partly because lately I keep forgetting it, something that I’m actually a bit ashamed, although it seems apparently to be quite normal among data scientists to forget some basic things, like reading CSV files. I suppose a background is needed. I first started to use R because I’m interested in its visualization capability.

item.title: Exactly what I needed this week
item.description: Je viens d’avoir ma femme me notifie que IU vient de publier une nouvelle chanson. Le titre est “Blueming” (comme un mélange de Blue et Blooming) et honnêtement je ne savais pas avant mais c’est exactement ce dont j’avais besoin pour passer cette semaine assez rugueuse. Aussi comment ça s’appelle ce genre de musique ? Cela a l’air super bon c’est comme les chansons depuis early 2000s. C’est très “homy” mais en même temps aussi énergétique.

item.title: Embedding video on Rmarkdown using Blogdown package
item.description: I’ve just read this article about embedding YouTube videos using this simple blogdown function: blogdown::shortcode("youtube", "P3aGk_Gwqjw") where "P3aGk_Gwqjw" is the video’s YouTube ID. And that’s all. No wasted lines; very efficient. I think I will never cease to be amazed by R developers. The possibilities are truly endless now. PS: “Electro swing”. Check it out on YouTube. Thank me later.

item.title: Leaflet Test
item.description: This is my first blog post featuring a leaflet map. I’ve followed the step-by-step guide provided in this blog post and apparently it works. To say that I’m happy would be an understatement. Now I’m able to write blog post with maps embedded in it. I’m going to look up how to “beautify” this website though; perhaps I’m going to find some custom themes.

item.title: About
item.description: My name is Ahmad Alkadri. You can find out more about me profesionally on my LinkedIn. Sometimes I travel and took photos. I visited Europe for the first time in 2008, specifically to Ukraine, and I fell in love with it. I came back in 2015, this time to France, for my Master study. Originally, I came from the field of Forestry. Due to some weird course of fate, I became entangled in the field of wood acoustics, wrote a scientific article about violin wood, and now I work as an Applied Researcher in the R&D department of Henri SELMER Paris.

item.title: First Post in this Blog
item.description: After many months considering it, and even started it but chickened because of the seeming complexities of it, I finally got around to putting this simple blog together. This site is powered by Hugo and edited completely in Rstudio. I still marvel at R these days. Doing statistics is one thing, finite difference is another, but making a package so that people can blog using R? I’ll continue writing here, mostly small notes, quotes, and some codes.
